response.justification: Candidate brings 20 years of solid full-stack engineering across diverse domains and current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure. However, the role demands director-level strategic leadership in AI with recognized deep expertise and demonstrated customer advisory influence. AI experience appears recent (2025 start at re:cinq) and infrastructure-focused rather than strategic-product focused. Lacks the preferred master's/PhD in AI and shows no evidence of influencing enterprise cloud product strategy, advising strategic customers on AI adoption, or driving technology roadmaps at the principal level this role requires.
strengths: 20 years of full-stack engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure with multiple staff-level roles demonstrating consistent technical depth and advancement
Current hands-on involvement with AI-agent infrastructure, MCP frameworks, and agentic workflows shows current technical alignment with AI innovation space
Strong track record of mentoring and inspiring teams through code katas, workshops, and knowledge-sharing initiatives with proven ability to launch and sustain real-world products
concerns: AI/ML expertise appears recent and infrastructure-focused rather than the established strategic AI expertise expected for a principal-level strategy role
No advanced degree in AI-related field and limited evidence of AI research, product strategy, or thought leadership in the field
No demonstrated experience advising strategic customers on AI/cloud adoption or influencing enterprise product roadmapping and technical direction at organizational level

key_factors.factor: Limited AI strategy depth and customer advisory experience at principal level
Strong 20-year engineering foundation with current AI infrastructure exposure
No advanced AI degree and recent rather than established AI expertise
